Horrible experience. Simply useless plugin. Context depletion doesn't display. The reply window bounces back and forth. Copying is practically impossible. UI glitches. The built-in chat is unusable.

plugin.name: GitHub Copilot - Your AI Pair Programmer

comment:
New bug in the latest update.

Whenever I open a new conversation or reopen the browser, it automatically edits the files even if the changes have already been saved and committed. I have to roll back all the time.

This plugin has always been problematic; every update has a new issue. Even though I have a PHP Storm license, I'm switching to VS Code.

plugin.name: GitHub Copilot - Your AI Pair Programmer

comment:
IDEA Version of this Plugin has a lot of bugs. In VS Code I found no problems.

Examples: -Terminal cannot be read, bot tells output is empty -Chat freezes waiting for terminal, although it is fininished -IDE freezes regularly for about 3 minutes when chat gets longer -Switching to Agent Mode and back takes multiple seconds -Agent stops working, telling your prompt is to long (*new since last update) -Cannot add other files or images anymore except text files within the workspace -IDE freezes if you scale the Chat Window -Chat Window scrolles if you try to copy text -Input vanishes if you accidentally hit a special button (don't know which, but really annoying) -No retries errors, agent stops completely for nothing.. -Auto Mode is a piece of shit, just uses Codex Model instead of Opus for planning -MCP Servers sometimes just don't get connected -New bugs with every update...

To use the best models, you need a subscription. Really seeking for an alternative..

comment: The GitHub Copilot plugin continues to freeze the workspace very often, even after allocating the memory to 6GB this issue still persists. This started with the recent update.

plugin.name: GitHub Copilot - Your AI Pair Programmer

comment:
Overall poor experience.

These issues make it a struggle to use practically:

  1. unable to search previous chats.
  2. unable to share chats with other devs.
  3. unable to even copy the chat transcript in any meaningful way.

Annoyance: 4. interaction dialog has scrollback for recently input prompts, but accidentally keying down on a new prompt deletes what you've just written.

Do the plugin devs actually use it themselves?

plugin.name: GitHub Copilot - Your AI Pair Programmer

comment: 感觉不好用的可以试试通义千问…

plugin.name: GitHub Copilot - Your AI Pair Programmer

comment:
Full of persistent bugs. After 6 months of use (since September 2025), I'm disappointed by the consistent technical issues:

  • Terminal outputs not returning to the agent
  • Frequent chat history loss
  • Scrolling and navigation issues
  • Severe performance problems (10-20 second IDE freezes on good hardware)
  • Limited features compared to competitors The lower price point is the only advantage, but these issues have driven me (crazy) to seek alternatives.

plugin.name: GitHub Copilot - Your AI Pair Programmer

comment:
This plugin is essential yet has so many broken features:

  • Constant lock ups of whatever Jetbrains IDE you're using. Jetbrains will flag this component as causing performance problems constantly.
  • Ignores whether you're in Agent mode or Ask mode (sometimes Agent requests will get spit into Chat instead of acted upon).

plugin.name: GitHub Copilot - Your AI Pair Programmer

comment: Completely broken with current version of DataGrip

plugin.name: GitHub Copilot - Your AI Pair Programmer

comment: Fundamentally broken.

plugin.name: GitHub Copilot - Your AI Pair Programmer

comment:
This is by far the worst agent experience I've had with any of the tools:

  • Every time I start a new chat, multiple old and unrelated files are opened in the editor
  • I don't see any option to completely disable the automatic "add file to context" feature. I have to turn them off manually every time. I do not want to automatically add files to the context. This has led to unrelated files being added to the context numerous times which is a huge waste of tokens and context space. Just give me an option to disable this feature entirely, especially in agent mode.
  • The agent is still unable to read the terminal output most of the time
  • For about a week the model's output is missing characters, making it almost impossible to properly read what the model is telling me
  • I do not see any option to show me how full the current context is. This is super relevant to effectively work with the agent
  • I did not manage to configure skills in a way where the agent picks them up reliably
  • The feature to accept/decline edits made by the agent triggers so many edge cases it's hilarious. Make the agent change a file and then make the agent move it using a terminal command? The Changes are gone. Numerous bugs with the accept/decline buttons not rendering properly or rendering on top of completely unrelated UI elements (even on top of other/unrelated applications)
  • Configuring auto accepts for certain terminal calls is extremely cumbersome and hard to debug. I have not found any documentation for that and was forced to enable intellij logs to find out what the problem was.
  • When asked to accept a terminal command, there is no easy way to say "allow all uses of...". I have to open the settings (which is a modal, btw. I can't change the settings and use the agent at the same time) and manually add some regex to the table of allowed terminal commands. This is super cumbersome.

plugin.name: GitHub Copilot - Your AI Pair Programmer

comment: How it's still so crap after so long is beyond me... Please introduce negative star reviews. 2 star average is too high.

plugin.name: GitHub Copilot - Your AI Pair Programmer

comment:
I think that Github Copilot is getting decent now. Everything works properly, but I'm still missing some features and there's some improvements that could help:

  1. In PLAN mode, will be much better if we can answer the questions selecting something, or a better way, because when I plan something and answer some questions, the agent tries to start editing, using tokens to it.
  2. We can see how many tokens the context are using, to know when its better to reset the context.
  3. Some way to natively interate loops and tasks. Idk how the PLAN or AGENT mode works, but now there's no clear way to track the progress.
  4. Sometimes it tries to access some access denied directory and tries to run "cat" commands, and this is bad. I tried a workaround, but its not the best way.

plugin.name: GitHub Copilot - Your AI Pair Programmer

comment: I am fed up with the constant technical glitches / pitfalls in Copilot; it’s making my work impossible. I have been using Copilot since mid 2025, and it worked perfectly. After new releases in 2026, it becomes impossible to use. Basically, it freezes DataSpell completely when I'm trying to open Copilot/or write something there, and the only solution is to kill DataSpell. Now I switched to the previous release of Copilot 1.5.62-243 together with DataSpell 2025.2.3, and it works more or less better, still running intensively CPU when I'm trying to write in Copilot chat.

plugin.name: GitHub Copilot - Your AI Pair Programmer

comment:
Overall useful but the main pain points are :

  • impossible to copy code from the prompt result with Ctrl-C (why ??!!), you have to hit right-click>Copy like a boomer.
  • no chat history or possibility to navigate through prompts (like a jetbrains evaluate Alt-F8 / arrows). This is particularly painful when the prompt issues an error and you have to resubmit but since your prompt is always deleted after a search you have to retype it from scratch. Of course there's no context history and if you just ask "please reprocess my previous prompt" it's completely lost...

plugin.name: GitHub Copilot - Your AI Pair Programmer

comment: Half of the basic functions stop working at random, and restarting the IDE helps. Edit mode falls off especially often. Adding files to the context was done by a sadist. Automatic file addition works every other time. Adding the current selection also doesn't always work. The feeling that the developers didn't even try to use the basic features of the plugin for at least one day.
